title: Christmas Creek
content:
В В В В Phantom streams were in the distance mocking lights of lake and pool
В В В В Ghosts of trees of soft green lustre groves of shadows deep and cool!
В В В В Yea, some devil ran before them changing skies of brass to blue,
В В В В Setting bloom where curse is planted, where a grass-blade never grew.
В В В В Six there were, and high above them glared a wild and wizened sun,
В В В В Ninety leagues from where the waters of the singing valleys run.
В В В В There before them, there behind them, was the great, stark, stubborn plain,
В В В В Where the dry winds hiss for ever, and the blind earth moans for rain!
В В В В Ringed about by tracks of furnace, ninety leagues from stream and tree,
В В В В Six there were, with wasted faces, working northwards to the sea!

В В В В .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .

В В В В Ah, the bitter, hopeless desert! Here these broken human wrecks
В В В В Trod the wilds where sand of fire is with the spiteful spinifex,
В В В В Toiled through spheres that no bird knows of, where with fiery emphasis
В В В В Hell hath stamped its awful mint-mark deep on every thing that is!
В В В В Toiled and thirsted, strove and suffered! This was where DecemberпїЅs breath
В В В В As a wind of smiting flame is on weird, haggard wastes of death!
В В В В This was where a withered moan is, and the gleam of weak, wan star,
В В В В And a thunder full of menace sends its mighty voices far!
В В В В This was where black execrations, from some dark tribunal hurled,
В В В В Set the brand of curse on all things in the morning of the world!

В В В В .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .

В В В В One man yielded then another then a lad of nineteen years
В В В В Reeled and fell, with English rivers singing softly in his ears,
В В В В English grasses started round him then the grace of Sussex lea
В В В В Came and touched him with the beauty of a green land by the sea!
В В В В Old-world faces thronged about him old-world voices spoke to him;
В В В В But his speech was like a whisper, and his eyes were very dim.
В В В В In a dream of golden evening, beaming on a quiet strand,
В В В В Lay the stranger till a bright One came and took him by the hand.
В В В В England vanished; died the voices; but he heard a holier tone,
В В В В And an angel that we know not led him to the lands unknown!

В В В В .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .

В В В В Six there were, but three were taken! Three were left to struggle still;
В В В В But against the red horizon flamed a horn of brindled hill!
В В В В But beyond the northern skyline, past a wall of steep austere,
В В В В Lay the land of light and coolness in an April-coloured year!
В В В В пїЅCourage, brothers!пїЅ cried the leader. пїЅOn the slope of yonder peak
В В В В There are tracts of herb and shadow, and the channels of the creek!пїЅ
В В В В So they made one last great effort haled their beasts through brake and briar,
В В В В Set their feet on spurs of furnace, grappled spikes and crags of fire,
В В В В Fought the stubborn mountain forces, smote down naked, natural powers,
В В В В Till they gazed from thrones of Morning on a sphere of streams and flowers.

В В В В Out behind them was the desert, glaring like a sea of brass!
В В В В Here before them were the valleys, fair with moonlight-coloured grass!
В В В В At their backs were haggard waste-lands, bickering in a wicked blaze!
В В В В In their faces beamed the waters, marching down melodious ways!
В В В В Touching was the cool, soft lustre over laps of lawn and lea;
В В В В And majestic was the great road Morning made across the sea.
В В В В On the sacred day of Christmas, after seven months of grief,
В В В В Rested three of six who started, on a bank of moss and leaf
В В В В Rested by a running river, in a hushed, a holy week;
В В В В And they named the stream that saved them named it fitly пїЅChristmas CreekпїЅ.
meaning: About: Henry Clarence Kendall was an important nineteenth century Australian poet.
